metadata.purpose: Non-negotiable constraints for HYROX training plan generation

principles.name: Aerobic Dominance
principles.constraint: Aerobic training volume must constitute ≥60% of total training volume

sources.ref: Frontiers PMC11994925: >60% of race time is running
Dan Plews (sP13NcbZcKI): '60% of the HYROX race is running'
Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): '5 aerobic days minimum per 7-day week'
Matt Fitzgerald (r_OYi6B0tjA): '70-80% of running volume in Zone 2'
Fathom Nutrition: 'Aerobic base 70-80% of running volume at Zone 2'

principles.name: VO2max Development Priority
principles.constraint: Training must include systematic VO2max development through threshold and interval work

sources.ref: Frontiers PMC11994925: VO2max ρ=-0.71 with finish time (strongest predictor)
HYROX Sports Science Report 2025: 'VO2 Max is the #1 predictor'
Rich Ryan (eHnvndpBNnM): 'Threshold training is the most important element'
Dan Plews (xUcSSpN2nqU): 'Managing the finite anaerobic work capacity (W Prime)'

principles.name: Progressive Overload
principles.constraint: Weekly volume increase must not exceed 10%. Progression via distance, weight, density, or recovery time reduction

sources.ref: Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): '10% safe weekly increase for total volume'
Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): 'small tangible jumps: 2.5km running, 5min Zone 4, 5lbs lift'
Standard exercise science principle (ACSM guidelines)

principles.name: Periodization Structure
principles.constraint: Plan must follow Base → Build → Peak → Taper structure with defined phase durations

sources.ref: RMR (d00x9-RK1to): General Preparation → Specific Preparation phases
Dan Plews (xUcSSpN2nqU): 'Far from event → General; Close to event → Specific'
WOD Science (8Gl9QDw2GIQ): Phase 1 Volume → Phase 2 Intervals → Phase 3 Race Sim
Fathom: Base(1-4) → Build(5-10) → Peak(11-14) → Taper(15-16)
Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): '8 to 16 weeks for a specific training block'

base.focus: aerobic capacity, movement quality, general strength

principles.validation_rule: plan has exactly 4 phases AND base_weeks + build_weeks + peak_weeks + taper_weeks == total_weeks

principles.name: Concurrent Training Management
principles.constraint: Strength and high-intensity endurance sessions must be separated by ≥6 hours. Endurance:Strength ratio 1:1 to 2:1

sources.ref: Wilson 2012 meta-analysis (1072 citations): interference effect confirmed
Frontiers PMC11994925: 'separate resistance and endurance into distinct sessions'
WOD Science (hxRA1gsNxIA): 'Separating endurance and strength by 6-24 hours'
Nick Bare (ZBltiCOAREc): 'Auto-regulation crucial for balancing concurrent training'

principles.name: Specificity (Compromised Running)
principles.constraint: Training must include running under fatigue from functional stations (compromised running)

sources.ref: Dan Plews (sP13NcbZcKI): 'Of course you need to do compromised running... total nonsense to skip it'
Hunter McIntyre (WXdyyCfQBeQ): 'Train to be fast when feeling terrible'
Frontiers PMC11994925: 'running under such circumstances could have negatively affected running economy'
Fathom: 'station-specific practice under cardiovascular fatigue is non-negotiable'

principles.name: Recovery and Supercompensation
principles.constraint: Deload every 3-4 weeks (30% volume/intensity reduction). Min 2 easy days between quality sessions. Taper 1-2 weeks pre-race

sources.ref: Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): 'every 3rd or 4th week taking about 30% off both intensity and volume'
Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): 'at least two non-quality days between quality sessions'
Dan Plews (sP13NcbZcKI): 'Farmer's effort — always finish with 2-3 reps in reserve'
Rich Ryan (EWLqwwoOsnc): 'taper: final 1-2 weeks'

principles.name: Intensity Distribution
principles.constraint: Follow polarized (80/20) or pyramid model. 80% Zone 1-2, 20% Zone 4-5. Minimize unstructured Zone 3

sources.ref: Seiler 2010: polarized model in elite endurance athletes
Rosenblat 2019 meta-analysis: polarized > threshold for VO2max
Matt Fitzgerald (r_OYi6B0tjA): 'Polarized 80/20 intensity distribution optimal for HYROX'
Dan Plews (sP13NcbZcKI): 'sub-threshold, lactate 3-3.5 not 4-5'

principles.nuance: Rich Ryan (jAThVWh3kfo) argues Zone 3 is valuable for mitochondrial adaptation. Disagreement exists but polarized has stronger research support.

anti_patterns.name: Strength-First Trap
anti_patterns.description: Treating HYROX like CrossFit and prioritizing max strength over aerobic capacity
anti_patterns.evidence: Frontiers: muscle mass (ρ=-0.03), HGS (ρ=0.09), resistance training volume (ρ=0.34) — all NOT significant predictors
anti_patterns.correction: Strength training serves aerobic performance, not the other way around

anti_patterns.name: Simulation Overload
anti_patterns.description: Doing full HYROX simulations every week instead of building aerobic base
anti_patterns.evidence: Triage Method: 'Doing more race-paced training when lacking aerobic foundation just reinforces fatigue'
anti_patterns.correction: Full simulations every 3-4 weeks maximum during build phase

anti_patterns.name: Zone 3 Black Hole
anti_patterns.description: Training mostly at moderate-hard intensity (Zone 3) — too hard to recover, too easy to improve
anti_patterns.evidence: Seiler research: elite athletes avoid Zone 3; Rosenblat meta-analysis: polarized > threshold
anti_patterns.correction: Go easy on easy days, hard on hard days. Minimize unstructured Zone 3

anti_patterns.name: Ignoring Running
anti_patterns.description: Spending most training time on stations while neglecting running development
anti_patterns.evidence: Frontiers: running time correlates with finish time; stations do NOT. 60%+ of race is running.
anti_patterns.correction: Running is the biggest performance lever. Minimum 3 dedicated run sessions per week

anti_patterns.name: No Recovery Protocol
anti_patterns.description: Training hard every day without deloads or easy days
anti_patterns.evidence: Rich Ryan: 'at least two non-quality days between quality sessions'; Dan Plews: 'consistency is the main thing'
anti_patterns.correction: Deload every 3-4 weeks. 2+ easy days between hard sessions.
